name: How to Search for Resources - 211info
description: There are two ways to find resources using the 211info website: the Search bar and Guided Search. Both the Search bar and Guided Search can be found on our website home page . You can also click “Get Help” at the top of this page. The document below will guide you through the steps to find the resources and information you are looking for: How to search using the 211info Website – English If you need more support or if a link on our website is not working correctly, please dial 211 (866-698-6155). Language interpreters are available to help you. Find additional ways to contact the 211info team on our Contact page .

name: Housing & Shelter - 211info
description: If you are struggling to pay rent or need access to temporary housing or shelter, help is available. 211info can connect you to resources to keep you and your family safe, warm, and sheltered. For Summer and Heat-Related Resources, use the category options below, or visit the Summer and Heat-Related Resources webpage . For Winter and Severe Weather-Related Resources, use the category options below, or visit the Winter and Severe Weather-Related Resources webpage . < New Search Homeless Coordinated Entry Systems Housing Related Coordinated Entry X Housing Related Coordinated Entry Organizations that are part of a community-wide, coordinated effort to identify, assess and find appropriate options for homeless individuals and families seeking housing or related services, and/or for those at imminent risk of becoming homeless. name: Youth Services - 211info
description: 211info’s Maternal and Child Health (MCH) Team provides specialized information, referrals, and follow-up support for individuals and family members of all genders, covering needs related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, and sexual and reproductive health. MCH Coordinators help people navigate prenatal and postpartum care, children’s health programs, breastfeeding support, WIC, home visiting programs, STI testing, and breast and cervical cancer screenings. They also help locate essential items like formula, diapers, cribs, and car seats. To connect with the MCH team, call 211, text “health” or “salud” to 898211, or email [email protected] . < New Search Youth Employment Programs Youth Business Programs X Youth Business Programs Programs that utilize volunteers from the teaching profession, business and industry to provide firsthand information about the business world for students and/or school-age children and youth and opportunities for them to learn about economic principles and the economy. name: Food - 211info
description: 211info provides information and referrals to more than 1,500 food resources across Oregon, including food pantries, farmers markets, community gardens, fresh food distribution, and summer food programs for youth. Thanks to a partnership with Oregon Department of Human Services, we have an in-house SNAP Access Advocate who can assist with questions and applications. To find out what food assistance programs you might be eligible for or to speak with our SNAP Access Advocate, call 211 . You can also text your zip code to 898211 or email [email protected] . This institution is an equal opportunity provider.
